summary_one_line: Profile must encode colorspace FOURCC 'XYZ ' at offset 0x14 and 'Lab ' at 0x10, so a==cmsSigXYZData && b==cmsSigLabData.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace signature at input offset 0x14 must be the FOURCC 'XYZ ' (58 59 5a 20) while the PCS/colorspace field at 0x10 reads 'Lab ' (4c 61 62 20), so the two compared colorspace signatures equal cmsSigXYZData and cmsSigLabData respectively, satisfying the compound equality gate at line 502.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal FOURCC bytes that make a==cmsSigXYZData && b==cmsSigLabData; the per-role overlay shows the true side of line 502 taken 20 times in W (T=20) and the on-branch hitcount is 121-187, whereas loser seeds never take it.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at 0x10 and 'XYZ ' (58 59 5a 20) at 0x14 in the opposite slots, so a==cmsSigRGBData not cmsSigXYZData; line 502's true side is never taken (T=0) and the branch stays blocked 24h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace intercepts the integer/string CMP comparing the parsed colorspace signature against the literals cmsSigXYZData and cmsSigLabData, logs the constant operand, finds the seed bytes matching the other operand, and substitutes the exact 4-byte FOURCC into the input — naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit the exact 4-byte literal by chance, so they stay blocked all 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a compound equality gate requiring a==cmsSigXYZData and b==cmsSigLabData.
Winner seeds encode 'XYZ' at offset 0x14-0x16 while loser seeds encode 'XYZ' there only as part of the RGB-in-PCS arrangement.
Winner seed bytes at offset 0x10 spell 'XYZ Lab' (the XYZ/Lab interchangeable colorspaces).
Loser seed bytes at offset 0x10 spell 'RGB XYZ', placing RGB (not XYZ) as the colorspace compared at the gate.
The winner takes the true side of line 502 (T=20) while the loser never does (T=0), confirming the loser lacks the required signatures.
cmplog and vpc add I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for an operand match and substitutes the other CMP operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive and value_profile have no I2SRandReplace mutator.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2093 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0b8368b936d0cbb9` and observed one I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 14 of the 15-level ancestor chain; the tool's trailing 'I2S-floor signal: 1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 14' line confirms an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in the winner's lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on the 4-byte 'XYZ '/'Lab ' FOURCC equality in which naive/value_profile resolve the gate as fast as cmplog/vpc (no I2S advantage), which would mean the literal-substitution mechanism is not what separates winners from losers.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact byte-to-signature mapping (offset 0x10 = PCS 'Lab ', offset 0x14 = colorspace 'XYZ ') is inferred from the ICC header layout and the byte diff rather than from instrumented source dataflow tracing the parsed fields back to those offsets.
